_schema.note: One record per (country x version). Themes NOT listed are scored 0 (absent) and need no evidence. Every listed theme has score >= 1 with a quote + rationale + confidence. For single-verse / no-gap anthems, as-written and as-sung records are identical. Quotes are English translations unless noted; 'translation-sensitive' flags scores that could swing on translation choice.

war_arms.quote: To arms, citizens, form your battalions
war_arms.rationale: explicit martial call; whole verse is a battle exhortation

blood_death.quote: Let an impure blood water our furrows
blood_death.rationale: literal blood imagery, central to the refrain build-up

enemy_threat.quote: ferocious soldiers ... come to cut the throats of your sons
enemy_threat.rationale: vivid othering of an invading enemy

land_nature.quote: water our furrows
land_nature.rationale: agricultural soil imagery, incidental

unity_brotherhood.quote: children of the Fatherland
unity_brotherhood.rationale: collective national address

glory_pride.quote: the day of glory has arrived

war_arms.quote: To arms, citizens, form your battalions

blood_death.quote: Let an impure blood water our furrows
blood_death.rationale: blood imagery sustained across verses

enemy_threat.quote: ferocious soldiers ... come to cut the throats of your sons

sacrifice.rationale: later verses invoke dying for the nation

freedom_liberty.quote: Liberty, cherished Liberty (v6)
freedom_liberty.rationale: liberty is the explicit cause in v6

land_nature.quote: water our furrows
land_nature.rationale: soil imagery

unity_brotherhood.quote: children of the Fatherland
unity_brotherhood.rationale: collective address

glory_pride.quote: the day of glory has arrived

war_arms.rationale: bombardment imagery, but descriptive of the flag surviving rather than a call to arms

enemy_threat.rationale: enemy present only by implication in v1

freedom_liberty.quote: the land of the free
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom as epithet, not developed

glory_pride.quote: the home of the brave

war_arms.quote: Then conquer we must, when our cause it is just (v4)
war_arms.rationale: v1 battle imagery + v4 martial resolve

blood_death.quote: the gloom of the grave (v3)
blood_death.rationale: death imagery directed at the enemy

enemy_threat.quote: No refuge could save the hireling and slave (v3)
enemy_threat.rationale: explicit othering of the British enemy in v3

deity.quote: And this be our motto: 'In God is our trust' (v4)

freedom_liberty.quote: the land of the free ... where freemen shall stand (v4)
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom developed across verses

glory_pride.quote: the home of the brave

deity.rationale: the anthem is a prayer to God; dominant

glory_pride.quote: happy and glorious

enemy_threat.quote: Scatter our enemies, and make them fall (v2)
enemy_threat.rationale: v2 is an explicit plea against enemies - not sung

war_arms.quote: make them fall ... Confound their politics (v2)

glory_pride.quote: happy and glorious

war_arms.quote: the sound of machine guns as our melody

blood_death.quote: the streams of generous blood being shed
blood_death.rationale: blood and martyrdom recur

enemy_threat.quote: O France, this is the day of reckoning

sacrifice.quote: whether to live or to die ... from our dead we shall build up a glory

freedom_liberty.quote: we have fought for our independence
freedom_liberty.rationale: anti-colonial liberation

glory_pride.quote: from our dead we shall build up a glory

land_nature.quote: on the high djebels
land_nature.rationale: mountains as backdrop

unity_brotherhood.quote: we swear ... an oath
unity_brotherhood.rationale: collective pledge

war_arms.quote: the sound of machine guns as our melody

blood_death.quote: Let it be written with the blood of martyrs
blood_death.rationale: blood/martyrdom recur

enemy_threat.quote: O France, this is the day of reckoning

sacrifice.quote: from our dead we shall build up a glory

freedom_liberty.quote: we have fought for our independence
freedom_liberty.rationale: liberation

land_nature.quote: on the high djebels
land_nature.rationale: mountains

unity_brotherhood.quote: we swear ... an oath
unity_brotherhood.rationale: collective pledge

monarch.quote: May your reign continue for a thousand ... generations
monarch.rationale: the entire (single-verse) anthem wishes the Emperor's reign endless life

land_nature.quote: until the tiny pebbles grow into massive boulders lush with moss
land_nature.rationale: nature imagery as metaphor for endurance

monarch.quote: May your reign continue for a thousand ... generations
monarch.rationale: single-verse anthem devoted to the Emperor's reign

land_nature.quote: until the tiny pebbles grow into massive boulders lush with moss
land_nature.rationale: nature metaphor

deity.quote: that God, the Lord, dwelleth in this land
deity.rationale: a hymn to God, dominant in every verse

land_nature.quote: When the alps glow bright with splendour
land_nature.rationale: Alpine nature imagery is the other pillar

deity.quote: Thou, O loving Father, ever near

land_nature.quote: When dark clouds enshroud the hills and grey mist the valley fills
land_nature.rationale: nature imagery across verses

unity_brotherhood.quote: Unity and justice and freedom for the German fatherland
unity_brotherhood.rationale: st3 (the only official stanza) leads on collective unity

freedom_liberty.quote: Unity and justice and freedom
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom is one of the three named virtues

glory_pride.quote: Flourish in the radiance of this happiness

land_nature.quote: German fatherland
land_nature.rationale: fatherland reference, not developed nature imagery

unity_brotherhood.quote: Unity and justice and freedom for the German fatherland
unity_brotherhood.rationale: st3 unity carries over

freedom_liberty.quote: Unity and justice and freedom
freedom_liberty.rationale: named virtue

glory_pride.quote: Germany, Germany above everything, above everything in the world (st1)
glory_pride.rationale: st1 supremacist national pride - NOT sung

land_nature.quote: From the Meuse to the Neman (st1)
land_nature.rationale: st1 defines territory by rivers/boundaries

wildcard.label: praise of German women, wine and song
wildcard.quote: German women, German loyalty, German wine and German songs (st2)
wildcard.rationale: domestic/cultural pride (women, wine, song) not captured by themes 1-10 - the wildcard case, and not sung

unity_brotherhood.quote: Woven from hundreds of flowers, we are one garland that's Nepali
unity_brotherhood.rationale: unity-in-diversity is the central metaphor

land_nature.quote: A shawl of nature's wealth unending ... the plains, hills and mountains tall
land_nature.rationale: geography and natural beauty

blood_death.quote: From the blood of the braves, a nation free
blood_death.rationale: literal blood of the fallen invoked as the foundation of the free nation; mild but explicit

sacrifice.quote: From the blood of the braves, a nation free
sacrifice.rationale: honours those who died for freedom

freedom_liberty.quote: Spread sovereign from Mechi to Mahakali ... a nation free
freedom_liberty.rationale: sovereignty / freedom mention

glory_pride.quote: This progressive nation of ours, all hail Nepal!

unity_brotherhood.quote: Woven from hundreds of flowers, we are one garland that's Nepali
unity_brotherhood.rationale: unity-in-diversity central

land_nature.quote: A shawl of nature's wealth unending ... the plains, hills and mountains tall
land_nature.rationale: geography/beauty

blood_death.quote: From the blood of the braves, a nation free

sacrifice.quote: From the blood of the braves, a nation free
sacrifice.rationale: honours those who died for freedom

freedom_liberty.quote: Spread sovereign from Mechi to Mahakali ... a nation free
freedom_liberty.rationale: sovereignty / freedom

glory_pride.quote: This progressive nation of ours, all hail Nepal!

deity.quote: Lord, bless our nation (Nkosi Sikelel' iAfrika = 'God bless Africa')
deity.rationale: opens as a hymn invoking God's blessing; dominant

unity_brotherhood.quote: Sounds the call to come together, and united we shall stand
unity_brotherhood.rationale: explicit unity call

freedom_liberty.quote: Let us live and strive for freedom
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom as aspiration

land_nature.quote: over our everlasting mountains
land_nature.rationale: brief landscape reference

records.note: war_arms scored 0 deliberately: 'Banish wars and strife' mentions war only as a plea to END it - lexical presence is not thematic belligerence.

deity.quote: Lord, bless our nation (Nkosi Sikelel' iAfrika = 'God bless Africa')

unity_brotherhood.quote: Sounds the call to come together, and united we shall stand
unity_brotherhood.rationale: unity call

freedom_liberty.quote: Let us live and strive for freedom
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom aspiration

land_nature.quote: over our everlasting mountains
land_nature.rationale: landscape reference

records.note: war_arms scored 0 deliberately: 'Banish wars and strife' is anti-war.

war_arms.quote: We shall march, Angolan fighters, in solidarity with oppressed peoples.
war_arms.rationale: explicit martial self-identification as 'fighters' marching, recurring with 'we shall fight'

blood_death.quote: Oh Fatherland, we salute your children who died for our Independence.
blood_death.rationale: single reference to the fallen dead of independence

enemy_threat.quote: We shall march, Angolan fighters, in solidarity with oppressed peoples.
enemy_threat.rationale: oblique othering implied by 'oppressed peoples' / oppressors, no named foe

freedom_liberty.quote: Forward, Angola! Revolution through the power of the People: A United Country, Freedom, One People, one Nation!
freedom_liberty.rationale: Freedom and independence are explicit, recurring goals

unity_brotherhood.quote: A United Country, Freedom, One People, one Nation!
unity_brotherhood.rationale: 'One People, one Nation' is an explicit unity refrain

glory_pride.quote: Let us raise our liberated voices for the glory of the peoples of Africa.
glory_pride.rationale: explicit invocation of glory and honouring heroes

wildcard.label: revolution / a dated historical event (Fourth of February)
wildcard.quote: Oh Fatherland, we shall never forget the heroes of the Fourth of February.
wildcard.rationale: the 1961 revolt date and 'Revolution' are a central organising motif not covered by other themes

war_arms.quote: We shall march, Angolan fighters, in solidarity with oppressed peoples.
war_arms.rationale: explicit martial self-identification as 'fighters' marching, recurring with 'we shall fight'

blood_death.quote: Oh Fatherland, we salute your children who died for our Independence.
blood_death.rationale: single reference to the fallen dead of independence

enemy_threat.quote: We shall march, Angolan fighters, in solidarity with oppressed peoples.
enemy_threat.rationale: oblique othering implied by 'oppressed peoples' / oppressors, no named foe

freedom_liberty.quote: Forward, Angola! Revolution through the power of the People: A United Country, Freedom, One People, one Nation!
freedom_liberty.rationale: Freedom and independence are explicit, recurring goals

unity_brotherhood.quote: A United Country, Freedom, One People, one Nation!
unity_brotherhood.rationale: 'One People, one Nation' is an explicit unity refrain

glory_pride.quote: Let us raise our liberated voices for the glory of the peoples of Africa.
glory_pride.rationale: explicit invocation of glory and honouring heroes

wildcard.label: revolution / a dated historical event (Fourth of February)
wildcard.quote: Oh Fatherland, we shall never forget the heroes of the Fourth of February.
wildcard.rationale: the 1961 revolt date and 'Revolution' are a central organising motif not covered by other themes

war_arms.quote: Knew how to engage in mighty battles With strength, courage, ardour
war_arms.rationale: verse 1 explicitly recalls ancestors waging 'mighty battles'

blood_death.quote: Livrer au prix du sang des combats éclatants.
blood_death.original: Livrer au prix du sang des combats éclatants.
blood_death.rationale: single mention that battles were waged 'at the price of blood'

enemy_threat.quote: When all around there blows a wind of anger and hate
enemy_threat.rationale: oblique external hostility, no named foe

freedom_liberty.quote: The resounding cry of freedom Is heard at the first light of dawn
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom is the chorus's central cry, repeated

land_nature.quote: Beloved Benin, your sunny mountains, palm trees, and green pastures Show everywhere your brightness; Your soil offers everyone the richest fruits.
land_nature.rationale: verse 3 is an explicit catalogue of the land's geography and bounty

unity_brotherhood.quote: Benin, from henceforth your sons are united With one brotherly spirit
unity_brotherhood.rationale: explicit fraternity in verses 1 and 3

glory_pride.quote: Citizen of Benin, be proud

wildcard.label: the national flag and its colours
wildcard.quote: In the green you read hope of spring; The red signifies the courage of your ancestors; The yellow foretells the richest treasures.
wildcard.rationale: verse 2 reads the tricolour flag's symbolism, a distinct motif

freedom_liberty.quote: The resounding cry of freedom Is heard at the first light of dawn
freedom_liberty.rationale: in verse 1 + chorus alone, the 'cry of freedom' is the dominant idea

war_arms.quote: Knew how to engage in mighty battles With strength, courage, ardour

blood_death.quote: Livrer au prix du sang des combats éclatants.
blood_death.original: Livrer au prix du sang des combats éclatants.
blood_death.rationale: verse 1 says battles waged 'at the price of blood'

unity_brotherhood.quote: Builders of the present, you too, join forces Each day for the task stronger in unity.
unity_brotherhood.rationale: verse 1 calls to join forces in unity

deity.quote: This land of ours, Is a gift from God
deity.rationale: the land is framed as God's gift; explicit divine reference

land_nature.quote: This land of ours, Is a gift from God, An inheritance from our forefathers
land_nature.rationale: the land/'Our Land' is the anthem's central subject across both verses

unity_brotherhood.quote: Let us work together to serve, Our land.
unity_brotherhood.rationale: explicit call to work together; verse 2 'bound together'

glory_pride.quote: Beautiful name of fame Of the nation of Botswana
glory_pride.rationale: single reference to the nation's renown

wildcard.label: labour / call to work
wildcard.quote: Be energized, Let us work together to serve, Our land.
wildcard.rationale: the chorus's rousing 'awake/arise and work' is a distinct labour motif

deity.quote: This land of ours, Is a gift from God
deity.rationale: verse 1 frames the land as God's gift; explicit divine reference

land_nature.quote: This land of ours, Is a gift from God, An inheritance from our forefathers
land_nature.rationale: in verse 1 + chorus the land is the sole dominant subject

unity_brotherhood.quote: Let us work together to serve, Our land.
unity_brotherhood.rationale: chorus is an explicit call to work together

wildcard.label: labour / call to work
wildcard.quote: Be energized, Let us work together to serve, Our land.
wildcard.rationale: the chorus's 'awake/arise and work' is a distinct labour motif

war_arms.quote: Had not only hand arms, but also and above all The flame in their hearts
war_arms.rationale: explicit reference to bearing arms by the revolutionary volunteers

blood_death.quote: Motherland or death, we shall conquer.
blood_death.rationale: the chorus's 'Motherland or death' is a recurring martyrdom/death refrain

enemy_threat.quote: Against the cynical malice transformed into neo-colonialism and its petty local servants
enemy_threat.rationale: explicit othering of neo-colonialists and their 'servants' polluting sovereignty

sacrifice.quote: Motherland or death, we shall conquer.
sacrifice.rationale: death-before-defeat pledge to the motherland is explicit

freedom_liberty.quote: In the acquisition of liberty and progress.
freedom_liberty.rationale: liberty/freeing Faso from 'fetters' is a recurring goal

unity_brotherhood.quote: One single night has brought together our people With all the peoples of the World
unity_brotherhood.rationale: explicit gathering of the people together

glory_pride.quote: And one single night has launched its triumphal march. Towards the horizon of good fortune.

wildcard.label: revolution (the 4 August revolution)
wildcard.quote: Nourished in the lively source of the Revolution, The volunteers for liberty and peace With their nocturnal and beneficial energies of the 4 of August
wildcard.rationale: Sankara's 1983/84 Revolution is the central organising frame of the whole text

blood_death.quote: Motherland or death, we shall conquer.
blood_death.rationale: chorus's 'Motherland or death' is a death-or-victory refrain in the sung text

sacrifice.quote: Motherland or death, we shall conquer.

war_arms.quote: And one single night has launched its triumphal march.
war_arms.rationale: the 'triumphal march' and 'we shall conquer' carry martial connotation in verse 1 + chorus

freedom_liberty.quote: In the acquisition of liberty and progress.
freedom_liberty.rationale: liberty is an explicit goal in the chorus

unity_brotherhood.quote: One single night has brought together our people With all the peoples of the World
unity_brotherhood.rationale: explicit gathering of the people together

glory_pride.quote: And one single night has launched its triumphal march. Towards the horizon of good fortune.

wildcard.label: revolution (one fateful night uniting the people)
wildcard.quote: And one single night has drawn together The history of an entire people
wildcard.rationale: the revolutionary 'single night' organises even the sung verse 1 + chorus

blood_death.quote: Wounded and bruised, you have remained master of yourself.
blood_death.rationale: oblique metaphor of the nation wounded/bruised, no literal blood or graves

sacrifice.quote: We vow to your noble service our hands and hearts and lives.
sacrifice.rationale: explicit pledge of 'lives' to the nation's service

deity.quote: May God, who gave you to us, keep you for us to venerate

freedom_liberty.quote: Sing the glory of liberty conquered again.
freedom_liberty.rationale: liberty/self-government recurs across verses

unity_brotherhood.quote: Under the shield of unity, In peace, joy and prosperity.
unity_brotherhood.rationale: single closing reference to unity

glory_pride.quote: Lifting yourself proudly into the ranks of free peoples.
glory_pride.rationale: honour/glory/pride recur ('sense of honour', 'sing the glory')

land_nature.quote: Beloved Burundi, gentle country
land_nature.rationale: single affectionate reference to the country, little concrete geography

freedom_liberty.quote: Sing, brother Sing, my brother For Liberty is a hymn And Man the certainty.
freedom_liberty.rationale: Liberty is the title and the dominant, recurring subject ('Chant of Liberty')

unity_brotherhood.quote: Sing, brother Sing, my brother
unity_brotherhood.rationale: the repeated address to 'brother' frames the song fraternally

land_nature.quote: Hope is as big as the sea Which embraces us Sentinel of the oceans and winds
land_nature.rationale: the island, sea and winds are explicit recurring imagery

wildcard.label: hope / resilience
wildcard.quote: Hope is as big as the sea Which embraces us
wildcard.rationale: hope and perseverance are a distinct organising sentiment

blood_death.quote: Their tears and blood and sweat thy soil did water
blood_death.rationale: single line on forefathers' blood watering the soil; ancestral 'repose'

land_nature.quote: From Shari, from where the Mungo meanders From along the banks of lowly Boumba Stream
land_nature.rationale: verse 2 catalogues named rivers and Buea Mountain; hills and valleys in verse 1

unity_brotherhood.quote: Muster thy sons in union close around thee
unity_brotherhood.rationale: explicit call to gather the nation's sons in union

glory_pride.quote: Land of Promise, land of Glory!
glory_pride.rationale: the chorus is built on honour, glory and devotion

wildcard.label: Pan-African loyalty
wildcard.quote: Foster, for Mother Africa, a loyalty That true shall remain to the last.
wildcard.rationale: loyalty to 'Mother Africa' is a distinct continental motif

blood_death.quote: Their tears and blood and sweat thy soil did water
blood_death.rationale: verse 1's single line on forefathers' blood watering the soil

land_nature.quote: On thy hills and valleys once their tillage rose.

glory_pride.quote: Land of Promise, land of Glory!

wildcard.label: ancestors / forefathers at rest
wildcard.quote: O Cameroon, Thou Cradle of our Fathers, Holy Shrine where in our midst they now repose
wildcard.rationale: verse 1 frames the land as a shrine to the resting forefathers

enemy_threat.quote: Long subjugated, long scorned by all, But, from today, breaking tyranny's hold.
enemy_threat.rationale: explicit othering of those who subjugated/scorned and of 'tyranny', recurring

freedom_liberty.quote: Take up again your right to respect, to life!
freedom_liberty.rationale: reclaiming rights / breaking tyranny's hold is an explicit liberation theme

unity_brotherhood.quote: Through work, order and dignity You reconquer your rights, your unity
unity_brotherhood.rationale: unity recurs in both the verse and refrain

glory_pride.quote: Holding high the flag of the Fatherland.

wildcard.label: labour / work as the path to renewal
wildcard.quote: To work! In order and dignity, In the respect for rights and in unity
wildcard.rationale: 'Au travail' / work in dignity is the refrain's organising idea, distinct from war

land_nature.rationale: single 'cradle of the Bantu' evocation, not concrete geography

freedom_liberty.quote: Your freedom will be born of your courage.

blood_death.rationale: 'one blood' used metaphorically for kinship, not literal bloodshed

land_nature.quote: On these Islands we were born, These Islands brought us up.

wildcard.quote: Love our religion and the world.
wildcard.rationale: shared faith/religion is an explicit recurring theme beyond a single deity invocation

war_arms.quote: Let us all fight, every one of us, For our old black country.
war_arms.rationale: verse 2 explicit call to fight; verse 3 'Triumph comes through battle'

blood_death.quote: And if we have to die, What does it really matter?
blood_death.rationale: verse 3 explicitly contemplates dying for the country

sacrifice.quote: And if we have to die, What does it really matter? Our children Everywhere will be able to say
sacrifice.rationale: verse 3 frames death as a worthwhile sacrifice for posterity

freedom_liberty.quote: Let us all, with wild joyfulness, sing The song of freedom.
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom/independence is an explicit theme of verse 1

unity_brotherhood.quote: Proclaim the unity of our nation. Let us forget what divides us And become more united than ever.
unity_brotherhood.rationale: the chorus is built around unity ('Unity, work, progress')

glory_pride.quote: One people, one soul, One heart, ardent and proud.
glory_pride.rationale: explicit national pride; 'Triumph comes through battle'

land_nature.quote: From the forest to the bush, From the bush to the ocean
land_nature.rationale: single geographic sweep across forest, bush and ocean

wildcard.label: labour / progress (national motto)
wildcard.quote: Let us live our motto: Unity, work, progress.
wildcard.rationale: the 'work, progress' motto is a distinct developmental motif

freedom_liberty.quote: Let us all, with wild joyfulness, sing The song of freedom.
freedom_liberty.rationale: in verse 1 + chorus, freedom/independence is the leading idea

unity_brotherhood.quote: Proclaim the unity of our nation. Let us forget what divides us And become more united than ever.
unity_brotherhood.rationale: the sung chorus is dominated by unity

glory_pride.quote: Arise, Congolese, proud every man
glory_pride.rationale: single call to national pride in the chorus

wildcard.label: labour / progress (national motto)
wildcard.quote: Let us live our motto: Unity, work, progress.
wildcard.rationale: the 'work, progress' motto appears in the sung chorus

deity.quote: Citizens, sing the sacred hymn Of your solidarity
deity.rationale: 'sacred' hymn/oath, but no explicit deity named (sacred = 1 at most)

freedom_liberty.quote: Sacred day of the immortal oath of freedom That we pass on To our posterity
freedom_liberty.rationale: freedom/independence is an explicit recurring goal

unity_brotherhood.quote: United by fate, United in the struggle for independence
unity_brotherhood.rationale: 'united by fate', solidarity recur prominently

glory_pride.quote: We will populate your soil And ensure your greatness.
glory_pride.rationale: pledge to the nation's greatness; 'Proudly Greet the golden emblem'

land_nature.quote: We will populate your soil And ensure your greatness.
land_nature.rationale: single reference to populating the soil

wildcard.label: labour / nation-building in peace
wildcard.quote: Oh ardent people, by hard work, We will build a country more beautiful than before, In peace.
wildcard.rationale: building the country through 'hard work' and explicitly 'In peace' is a distinct, repeated motif
